Apple___Apple_scab.description: Fungal disease caused by Venturia inaequalis. Dark, scaly lesions on leaves and fruit.
symptoms: Olive-green to brown spots on leaves
prevention: Apply dormant sprays in early spring

Apple___Black_rot.description: Fungal disease caused by Botryosphaeria obtusa. Causes fruit rot and leaf spots.
prevention: Maintain tree vigor with proper fertilization
spread_conditions: Wounds or insect damage on fruit

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.description: Fungal disease requiring both apple and cedar/juniper trees to complete lifecycle.
symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surface
treatment: Begin sprays at pink bud stage
Repeat every 7-10 days during wet weather
prevention: Remove nearby cedar and juniper trees

Apple___healthy.description: Plant appears healthy with no signs of disease.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.description: Fungal disease caused by Cercospora zeae-maydis. One of the most yield-limiting diseases of maize.
symptoms: Rectangular gray to tan lesions parallel to leaf veins
prevention: Reduce plant density for air circulation

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.description: Fungal disease caused by Puccinia sorghi. Visible as brick-red pustules on leaves.
symptoms: Small circular to elongated brick-red pustules on both leaf surfaces
Pustules turn dark brown-black as they mature
Severe infection causes yellowing and leaf death
treatment: Begin treatment at first sign of pustules
prevention: Early planting to avoid peak rust season

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.description: Fungal disease caused by Exserohilum turcicum. Major yield-reducing disease worldwide.
symptoms: Long cigar-shaped gray-green to tan lesions
Lesions 2.5-15cm long parallel to leaf margins
treatment: Use Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/L as protective spray
Treat at tasseling stage for best results
prevention: Use resistant hybrid varieties — most important control
Rotate with non-host crops like soybean or wheat
Deep plow to bury infected residue

Corn_(maize)___healthy.description: Plant appears healthy with no signs of disease.
prevention: Monitor weekly for early disease signs

Grape___Black_rot.description: Fungal disease caused by Guignardia bidwellii. Can destroy entire grape crop if untreated.
symptoms: Tan circular spots with dark borders on leaves
Infected berries shrivel and turn black
treatment: Begin spraying at bud break, repeat every 10-14 days

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).description: Complex fungal disease affecting wood and leaves. One of the most destructive grapevine diseases.
symptoms: Tiger-stripe pattern on leaves — yellow/red between veins
Small dark spots on berries (black measles)
treatment: Remove and burn severely affected vines
Apply sodium arsenite to pruning wounds (where legal)
prevention: Protect pruning wounds with fungicide paste

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.description: Fungal disease caused by Isariopsis clavispora. Affects leaves and reduces photosynthesis.
symptoms: Irregular dark brown spots on older leaves
prevention: Improve air circulation by proper pruning

Grape___healthy.description: Plant appears healthy with no signs of disease.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.description: Bacterial disease caused by Xanthomonas arboricola pv. pruni. Affects leaves, fruit, and twigs.
symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves turning purple-brown
Shot-hole appearance as spots fall out
treatment: Begin at petal fall, repeat every 10-14 days
prevention: Avoid wounding trees during wet weather

Peach___healthy.description: Plant appears healthy with no signs of disease.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.description: Bacterial disease caused by Xanthomonas campestris pv. vesicatoria. Common in warm humid regions.
symptoms: Small water-soaked circular spots on leaves
Spots turn brown with yellow halo
Premature leaf drop reducing fruit quality
treatment: Repeat every 7-10 days in wet weather
prevention: Remove and destroy infected plant debris

Pepper,_bell___healthy.description: Plant appears healthy with no signs of disease.
prevention: Balanced fertilization with potassium for disease resistance

Potato___Early_blight.description: Fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani. Reduces yield by killing leaves prematurely.
symptoms: Dark brown circular spots with concentric rings (target board pattern)
treatment: Treat every 7-10 days from 30 days after planting
prevention: Maintain adequate plant nutrition especially nitrogen
spread_conditions: Plant stress from drought or poor nutrition

Potato___Late_blight.description: Caused by Phytophthora infestans. Most devastating potato disease — caused Irish Famine 1845.
symptoms: Water-soaked pale green spots on leaf edges
White fuzzy growth on leaf underside in humid conditions
treatment: Spray every 5-7 days in cool wet weather
prevention: Use certified resistant varieties like Kufri Jyoti
Hill up soil around plants to protect tubers

Potato___healthy.description: Plant appears healthy with no signs of disease.
prevention: Monitor for late blight weekly in cool wet weather

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.description: Bacterial disease caused by Xanthomonas species. Affects leaves, stems, and fruit.
symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves turning brown
Raised scabby spots on green fruit
treatment: Apply every 7 days in wet conditions
prevention: Rotate crops — avoid tomato family for 2 years

Tomato___Early_blight.description: Fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani. Very common in warm regions.
symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings on older leaves
Lesions on stem at soil level (collar rot)
treatment: Begin at first sign, repeat every 7-10 days

Tomato___Late_blight.description: Caused by Phytophthora infestans. Can destroy entire crop in 7-10 days in favourable conditions.
symptoms: Irregular water-soaked gray-green spots on leaves
treatment: Spray every 5-7 days — do not delay

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.description: Fungal disease caused by Passalora fulva. Common in greenhouse and humid field conditions.
symptoms: Pale green-yellow spots on upper leaf surface
Olive-green to brown velvety mold on leaf underside
prevention: Reduce humidity by improving air circulation
Remove lower leaves to improve airflow

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.description: Fungal disease caused by Septoria lycopersici. Very common and destructive in wet seasons.
symptoms: Small circular spots with dark brown border and gray center
Tiny black dots (pycnidia) visible in spot center
treatment: Remove and destroy infected leaves before spraying

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.description: Infestation by Tetranychus urticae. Not a fungal disease — caused by tiny mites sucking sap.
symptoms: Tiny yellow or white stippling on leaves
Leaves turn bronze then dry out
Severe: entire plant looks dusty and dying
treatment: Spray on leaf underside — repeat every 5-7 days
prevention: Maintain adequate soil moisture — dry conditions favor mites

Tomato___Target_Spot.description: Fungal disease caused by Corynespora cassiicola. Affects all above-ground plant parts.
symptoms: Circular brown spots with concentric target-like rings
Spots on leaves, stems, and fruit
Dark brown sunken lesions on fruit

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.description: Viral disease caused by Tomato mosaic virus (ToMV). Spreads through contact and tools.
symptoms: Mosaic pattern of light and dark green on leaves
treatment: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
prevention: Control aphid population with neem oil

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.description: Viral disease transmitted by whitefly Bemisia tabaci. Devastating in tropical regions including Telangana.
symptoms: Upward curling and yellowing of leaves
Flower drop and very poor fruit set
treatment: No cure — remove infected plants to prevent spread
Spray Imidacloprid 17.8% SL @ 0.5ml/L to kill whitefly vectors
Yellow sticky traps to monitor whitefly
prevention: Use TYLCV-resistant varieties like Arka Rakshak
Rogue out infected plants in first 3 weeks
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.severity: Very High — major problem in Telangana
spread_conditions: Lack of natural enemies for whitefly

Tomato___healthy.description: Plant appears healthy with no signs of disease.
prevention: Apply balanced fertilizer — avoid excess nitrogen
